text: There is something ancient and true in the terror you feel — it is the body's way of confirming that what you are about to do actually matters, that you have stepped close enough to the edge of your own becoming to feel the vertigo of genuine possibility. Every worthwhile thing was once somebody's terrifying threshold. The caterpillar does not find metamorphosis comfortable; it liquefies entirely before it flies. What you carry into this new space is not only skill or preparation but something harder to name — the accumulated weight of every moment you chose to keep going when the easier path glittered beside you. That history does not vanish at the threshold. It walks with you. Whatever shape this venture takes, whatever reversals and surprises await in the unmarked territory ahead, you are already the kind of person who decided to begin. That is not nothing. That is, in fact, almost everything.
